title: Dry Brined Japanese Beef Gyudon
description: A fresh and juicy Japanese dish featuring dry brined beef, served over rice in a gyudon style, perfect for a lazy Sunday
instructions: In a small bowl, mix together kosher salt, sugar, and black pepper to create the dry brine.
Place the beef slices in a single layer on a wire rack set over a rimmed baking sheet or a tray.
Sprinkle the dry brine evenly over the beef slices, making sure each piece is coated.
Let the beef sit at room temperature for 30 minutes to allow the dry brine to work.
After 30 minutes, rinse the beef slices under cold running water to remove the dry brine, then pat dry with paper towels.
Heat 1 tsp of sesame oil in a large tava griddle over medium-high heat.
Add the beef slices to the griddle and cook for 3-4 minutes per side, or until cooked to your desired level of doneness.
While the beef is cooking, mix together soy sauce, sake, and mirin in a small bowl.
Once the beef is done, brush the soy sauce mixture evenly over the beef slices.
To assemble the gyudon, place a scoop of cooked Japanese rice onto a plate or bowl, then top with a few slices of the beef and some green onions.
